title: Bad Drives Out Good

content.statement: When buyers can't see quality and the price won't move, bad drives out good.
content.explainer: If a fixed price can't reward better quality and customers can't tell the difference before buying, the honest seller's extra effort goes unpaid, so quality quietly exits the market. Hidden quality plus rigid pricing is how good options get competed out by cheaper fakes.
questions: Where do buyers in your market pay one price but can't tell your quality from a cheaper fake?
Is your best work being undercut because customers can't see the difference before they buy?
What signal, guarantee, or proof could make your quality visible enough to command its price?
sop: Spot a market where you sell at a fixed or expected price and buyers can't verify quality upfront.
Rate how hidden your quality is to buyers before they purchase, from 1 to 10 (see calculation, page 6).
Rate how rigid the price is, how little it can flex to reward better quality, from 1 to 10 (see calculation, page 6).
Multiply the two scores to gauge how strongly bad offerings will drive out good ones (see calculation, page 6).
Attack a high score with visible proof, guarantees, transparency, reviews, credible signals, so quality can command its price.

definitions.def: When one side of a deal knows more about quality than the other, and that hidden information pushes the good options out of the market.
When a price is fixed or slow to move, so it can't adjust to reflect real differences in quality between sellers.
Originally about money. When two things trade at one fixed value but differ in real worth, people pass on the worse one and hold back the better.

content.equation: Adverse Selection = Quality Opacity × Price Rigidity
content.equation_example: Quality Opacity 8 × Price Rigidity 7 = 56 out of 100. A high score means buyers can't tell good from bad at a fixed price, so sellers of quality quit the market; a low score means quality can command its price and survive.
content.closing_statement: In a world that hides quality, the honest move is to make yours impossible to fake.
content.question: Where do buyers in your market pay one price but can't tell your quality from a cheaper fake?

caption:
Principle Nº 341: Bad Drives Out Good

When buyers can't see quality and the price won't move, bad drives out good.

Ask yourself: Where do buyers in your market pay one price but can't tell your quality from a cheaper fake?
